meta.subject: Mathematics
notation: Use the 'therefore' symbol (∴) before final answers
Use decimal point (not comma) for decimals
Coordinates written as (x; y) with semicolon
markingStandards: Full marks require all working shown
meta.assessmentFormat: Paper 1: Algebra (expressions, equations and inequalities), Number patterns, Exponents and surds, Functions and graphs, Finance, growth and decay, Probability. Paper 2: Trigonometry, Euclidean Geometry and Measurement, Analytical Geometry, Statistics.

subtopics.name: Algebraic Expressions
skills: Simplify algebraic expressions using the distributive law and laws of exponents
Multiply binomials and trinomials
Factorise algebraic expressions (common factor, difference of squares, trinomials of the form ax² + bx + c where a=1)
Simplify algebraic fractions (addition, subtraction, multiplication and division)
methods: Distributive law for expansion
Grouping and common factors for factorisation
Find LCD for algebraic fractions
commonSteps: Identify and factorise common factors or patterns
keyFormulae: (a + b)(a + c) = a² + ac + ab + bc

subtopics.name: Exponents and Surds
skills: Apply the laws of exponents for integral exponents (including negative exponents)
Simplify expressions involving exponents
Rationalise denominators and simplify surds
Estimate between which two integers a simple surd lies
Round rational and irrational numbers appropriately
methods: Laws of exponents: product, quotient, power, zero and negative exponents
Rationalise by multiplying by conjugate

subtopics.name: Equations and Inequalities
skills: Solve linear equations (including with brackets and fractions)
Solve quadratic equations by factorisation
Solve literal equations
Solve linear inequalities and represent solutions on a number line
Solve simultaneous linear equations (by substitution or elimination)
methods: Balancing method and inverse operations
Factorise and apply zero product rule for quadratics

subtopics.name: Number Patterns
skills: Investigate and extend number patterns (linear and quadratic)
Determine the general term (Tn) for linear patterns
Make conjectures and generalisations about patterns
Provide explanations and justifications
methods: Look for constant difference (linear) or constant second difference (quadratic)

subtopics.name: Functions
skills: Work with relationships between variables in numerical, graphical, verbal and symbolic forms
Sketch and interpret graphs of linear functions (y = ax + q), quadratic (y = ax² + q), hyperbolic (y = a/x + q) and exponential (y = abˣ + q, b > 0)
Determine the effect of parameters a and q on graphs
Find equations of graphs from given information
methods: Point-by-point plotting or use of key features (intercepts, turning points, asymptotes)
commonSteps: Identify the form of the function

subtopics.name: Finance and Growth
skills: Solve problems involving simple interest (A = P(1 + ni))
Solve problems involving compound growth and decay (A = P(1 + i)ⁿ)
Apply to contexts such as interest, hire purchase, inflation and population growth
methods: Use simple and compound growth/decay formulae
commonSteps: Identify the type of growth (simple or compound)

subtopics.name: Trigonometry
skills: Define trigonometric ratios (sin θ, cos θ, tan θ) in right-angled triangles
Extend definitions to 0° ≤ θ ≤ 360°
Use special angles (0°, 30°, 45°, 60°, 90°)
Solve simple trigonometric equations
Sketch graphs of sin θ, cos θ and tan θ
Solve problems in two dimensions using trigonometry
methods: SOH-CAH-TOA in right triangles
Use calculator for values
commonSteps: Solve using inverse functions or known values

subtopics.name: Euclidean Geometry
skills: Investigate and prove theorems on triangles (including similarity and congruence)
Apply properties of quadrilaterals
Solve geometric problems using theorems and riders
Use constructions to investigate properties
methods: Prove similarity (AA, SSS, SAS) and congruence (SSS, SAS, ASA, AAS, RHS)
Use angle properties and parallel lines

subtopics.name: Analytical Geometry
skills: Use the distance formula between two points
Use the gradient (slope) formula
Use the midpoint formula
Determine the equation of a straight line (gradient-intercept or point-gradient form)
Solve problems involving points, lines and triangles on the Cartesian plane
methods: Distance: √[(x₂ - x₁)² + (y₂ - y₁)²]
Gradient: m = (y₂ - y₁)/(x₂ - x₁)
Midpoint: ((x₁ + x₂)/2 ; (y₁ + y₂)/2)

subtopics.name: Measurement
skills: Calculate volume and surface area of right prisms, cylinders, pyramids, cones and spheres
Solve problems involving composite solids
Convert between SI units for length, area and volume
methods: Volume of prism/cylinder = base area × height
Surface area as sum of all faces

subtopics.name: Statistics
skills: Organise and summarise ungrouped and grouped data
Calculate measures of central tendency (mean, median, mode)
Calculate measures of dispersion (range, quartiles, interquartile range)
Draw and interpret box-and-whisker plots
Analyse and interpret statistical data
methods: Mean = Σx / n or for grouped data
Median from cumulative frequency or ordered data
commonSteps: Organise data into frequency tables if grouped

subtopics.name: Probability
skills: Use the fundamental counting principle
Calculate probability for single and combined events
Use two-way tables and tree diagrams for compound events
Solve probability problems in context
methods: P(A) = favourable / total
For independent events: P(A and B) = P(A) × P(B)
commonSteps: Use diagrams or tables where helpful
notation: P(A) for probability of event A
